title: The result of LAG/LEAD is non-deterministic value when offset is null
description:
### What happens?

The result of LAGпјЊLEAD is random value when offset is null.

```sql
select c1, c3, c2, lag(c3, c2,BITSTRING'010101010') over (partition by c1 order by c3) from test1 order by c1;
```

### To Reproduce

```sql
create table test1(c1 int, c2 smallint, c3 BITSTRING);
insert into test1 values(0, null, null);
insert into test1 values(1, 32767, '101');
insert into test1 values(2, -32767, '101');
insert into test1 values(3, 0, '000');
insert into test1 values(4, null, null);
select c1, c3, c2, lag(c3, c2,BITSTRING'010101010') over (partition by c1 order by c3) from test1 order by c1;
```

key_bug_features: LAG/LEAD returns random value when offset is null
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test2 (c1 INT, c2 SMALLINT, c3 BITSTRING, c4 VARCHAR(10));
INSERT INTO test2 VALUES (0, NULL, NULL, 'test1');
SELECT c1, c3, c2, LAG(c3, c2, BITSTRING '010101010') OVER (PARTITION BY c1 ORDER BY c3) FROM test2 ORDER BY c1;
